source_jobs.title: Director of Product Marketing
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.


ABOUT THE ROLE

Ziplines Education is at a point where the rigor of our product portfolio demands equally rigorous positioning behind it. As we scale the number of programs we launch and manage, how we articulate what each program is for — and who it's for — becomes one of the highest-leverage things we can get right. This role exists because that work is consequential enough to deserve a dedicated owner.

As Director of Product Marketing & Communications, you will own positioning across our active and pipeline programs, build and lead a systematic voice-of-customer practice, and serve as the strategic direction-setter for our marketing copy. The work is roughly 75% marketing communications and 25% product. The voice-of-customer research is the Product anchor that keeps positioning grounded in reality rather than assumption.

This role reports to the VP of Portfolio Strategy and works closely with the CEO, Growth, and Learning Experience Design (LXD) teams. You will also manage our Product Communications Manager, who handles project coordination and operational execution on your behalf. This is a role for someone who has strong opinions about what good positioning looks like, can edit copy with real instinct, and understands how to direct and enable others with a system rather than doing everything themselves.

 
 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Positioning & Strategy

 - Own positioning for all active and pipeline programs — develop defensible, specific positioning that reflects a real POV on why a program wins in market, not a summary of what it covers.

 - For each new launch, develop a clear target audience definition, core message, and expected learner profile that give Growth a strategic foundation before campaign planning begins.

 - Own company-wide ICP clarity across the existing portfolio — maintain a living, data-informed view of who our learners are, what drives them, and how that is evolving.

 - Monitor positioning health for existing programs. Flag when enrollment demographics, NPS signals, or performance patterns suggest positioning has drifted and a refresh is needed.

Voice of Customer

 - Own the VoC program — design and run a regular cadence of interviews with prospective learners, active learners, rejecters, and alumni.

 - Synthesize findings into clear positioning inputs and share them with Growth and Product on a defined cadence. Interviews that don't change something aren't worth running.

 - Partner with our data team to connect qualitative VoC findings with quantitative signals — enrollment demographics, NPS, ad performance — to build a complete picture of whether our positioning is working.

 - Run quarterly persona reviews to assess whether the learners we are reaching match the learners we are targeting.

Messaging Framework & Copy Quality

 - Serve as the primary strategic point of contact for our copywriter — provide clear positioning direction before he writes, and precise editorial direction when his output needs refinement. You are not writing everything yourself; you are making sure what he produces is right.

 - When VoC work or feedback loops from Growth signal that positioning has shifted, drive that change through the messaging framework and brief the copywriter on what needs to be updated in the messaging bank. You own the signal; he owns the pen.

 - Own the quarterly messaging bank review cycle: assess whether copy in marke

source_jobs.title: Ad Production Designer (Contract, Part-Time)
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.


ABOUT THE ROLE

 

Type: Hourly contract

Hours: ~15–25 hrs/week (variable)

Rate: $50–70/hr depending on motion proficiency and experience

Location: Remote



We're a fast-moving growth team running paid social campaigns across 30+ higher education partners. We have a strong creative foundation: brand guidelines, Figma templates, design systems and we need someone to help us produce at scale.

This is an execution-focused role. You won't be starting from a blank canvas. You'll be working inside existing templates and systems, iterating quickly on ad creative, and ensuring every partner's ads are polished and on-brand. If you're the kind of designer who thrives on throughput, precision, and shipping (not debating brand philosophy) this is a great fit.

The role is part-time and hourly with flexible weekly volume. There's room to grow into a bigger motion and video footprint as we expand into Reels and short-form formats.

 


WHAT YOU'LL BE DOING

Ad Production

 - Take creative briefs and produce ad variations across static formats (feed, stories, Reels covers) using existing Figma templates

 - Resize, adapt, and iterate on existing ads with new copy treatments, image swaps, format adjustments

 - Hit roughly 10–20 ad variants per week

 - Adapt master ad templates for 30+ partner brands using a custom Figma plugin (auto-populates brand colors, fonts, logos based on layer naming)

 - Review and QA output, adjusting spacing and typography where partner brand rules require it

 - Make edits on specific partner variations when requested

Motion/Video (Future state - in coming months)

 - Produce simple motion treatments and animated statics for Reels and short-form ads based on creative briefs and templates

 - Adapt master ad templates for 30+ partner brands

 - Review and QA output, adjusting spacing and typography where partner brand rules require it

 - Make edits on specific partner variations when requested

 


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

Must-haves

 - Solid Figma skills not just design, but precision in layer naming, component structure, and file hygiene (automation depends on it)

 - Experience producing social media ads, ideally in a performance marketing or agency context

 - Comfortable working from a brief that isn't always perfectly specified. You ask the right questions and move

 - Fast and organized. You can manage multiple active projects without things falling through the cracks

 - An eye for detail on typography and spacing, especially when adapting layouts for different brand constraints

 - Motion-capable from day one: comfortable producing simple animated statics and short-form motion treatments for Reels and feed

Nice-to-haves

 - Experience with Meta ads specifically. You know what a good Reels creative looks like vs. a feed ad

 - Familiarity with design ops workflows or multi-brand templating systems

 - Comfort working across time zones with overlap into our buying team's hours

To ensure a fair and consistent hiring process, we kindly ask that all applicants submit their applications through our job board rather than reaching out directly via LinkedIn, phone, or email. This helps our recruitment team review every application efficiently and equitably. We truly appreciate your interest in joining our team and look forward to considering your application!



Ziplines, Inc. is proud to be an equal-opportunity employer. We do not

source_jobs.title: Subject Matter Expert (SME) - AI for Project Management (Late 2026)
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.

Course development schedule

This course is being considered for development in the second half of 2026. We will be recruiting for SME roles on a rolling basis from now until the course enters development, so if you think you're a fit for the role, please fill out an application and we'll be in touch.

More about this AI for Project Management course

Project managers are under growing pressure to do more with less—faster timelines, tighter budgets, and higher complexity. AI offers a powerful way to reduce manual overhead, improve project visibility, and unlock more strategic thinking. This 5-week course is designed to help project and program managers learn how to use generative AI, automation tools, and AI-assisted planning to elevate their impact.

Learners will apply AI tools to real-world PM workflows—think project planning, timeline estimation, stakeholder updates, and risk tracking. By the end, learners will leave with practical strategies, reusable templates, and the confidence to lead AI-informed projects in any industry.

About you 

You bring at least 7 years of project management experience, at least 2+ years of experience in AI adoption, and a genuine passion for helping professionals thrive and achieve success. You have a point of view on what project managers need to succeed in applying AI to their work, and you’re able to break concepts down and make them accessible to beginners. As a SME for our online course module, you'll have the opportunity to shape our AI for Project Management curriculum, ensure we’re teaching what matters most, share your expertise with career advancers, and guide them toward achieving their goals. 



SME role objectives

 - Create compelling, relevant, industry-aligned courses that give learners what they need to advance in their field

 - Record authentic, engaging videos illustrating how to apply AI in project management.

 - Inspire learners, build confidence in their capabilities, and help them understand how to adopt and integrate AI into their project management practice.

 - Optimize learner engagement, learner retention, and learner success 



Our learners

Our learners are driven professionals from diverse fields. Some are just beginning to explore new career opportunities, while others are looking to deepen their expertise. What they all share is a strong desire to acquire new skills that will help them advance their careers—whether that’s stepping into a new role, earning a promotion, or excelling in their current position.



Our courses

Each week, learners:

 - Complete on-demand online content created by industry experts (that’s you!) featuring scenario-based learning, industry context, expert videos, and best practices (6-7 hours)

 - Apply their skills via a hands-on “Playbook” project that you help create (1-2 hours)

 - Attend a live session to learn from a different industry expert alongside their classmates (2 hours)

 - Get extra support by connecting with classmates on Slack or reaching out to our Learner Success team for support 



As a SME, you’ll collaborate with our Learning Experience Design team to shape the on-demand content and the Playbook, ensuring that the topical coverage, scenarios, videos, and applied skills are relevant and industry-aligned. Our designers will also ask for your input and review on the live session agenda. Most importantly, you’ll record authentic, engaging

source_jobs.title: Live Session Instructor - AI Automation
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.

About the Course

AI Automation equips learners with the knowledge and skills to design, implement, and manage real-world AI automation workflows using no-code and low-code tools. Weekly topics include:

 - Introduction to AI automation
   
   - Why use standard automation vs AI automation?
   
   - Real business cases
   
   - Demoing live automations on Make.com http://Make.com

 - Workflow design and mapping

 - AI integrations with business tools and applications
   
   - Lead hands-on demos and exercises showcasing automations built in Make.com http://Make.com, Zapier, and n8n, integrating tools like Slack, Telegram, Google Sheets, and other commonly used business applications.

 - Automation in sales, HR, customer support, and operations

 - Prompt engineering and AI agent collaboration
   
   - Showing example of Agentic AI and using AI agents in automations

 - Ethical implications and implementation strategies
   
   - Change management and AI governance
   
   - Optimizing, scaling, and presenting an AI-powered automation

About You

You bring:

 - 7+ years of hands-on experience in AI automation with a strong focus on real-world business applications.

 - Proven ability to build, troubleshoot, and present end-to-end automation workflows using tools like Make.com http://Make.com, Zapier, n8n, and NotebookLM—bringing concrete examples into live instructional sessions.

 - Expertise in leveraging AI tools such as OpenAI’s ChatGPT Plus to enhance automation and workflow design.

 - Experience guiding learners through complex automation challenges with clarity and confidence, especially in Zoom-based, hands-on learning environments.

 - Passion for mentoring professionals from diverse backgrounds, with a focus on skill-building and career advancement.

 - Skilled in process mapping with tools like Miro to visually design and communicate system flows and automation logic.

 - Ability to integrate popular business tools and platforms—including Slack, Telegram, X (formerly Twitter), and Google Sheets—into automation workflows.

 - Comfortable using tools like Gamma to create AI-enhanced presentations and documentation that support instruction and learner understanding.

 - Actively engaged in staying up to date on AI automation trends and best practices, with a commitment to making these technologies accessible and practical for all professionals.

Instructor Role Objectives

 - Fully complete the course in advance, gaining hands-on experience with all tools, workflows, and assignments to provide expert-level support to learners throughout the program.

 - Help learners apply automation skills through hands-on projects, guided builds, and real-world use cases.

 - Support learners in troubleshooting, refining, and expanding on the course’s core workflows by drawing from your own experience building them.

 - Inspire learners, build confidence, and foster a strong practical understanding of AI-powered workflows.

 - Drive engagement, retention, and learner success through active, thoughtful facilitation in a highly interactive, application-based learning environment.

Our Learners

Our learners are ambitious professionals from a wide range of industries, primarily in non-technical business roles. They are motivated to stay current with advancements in AI and automation, with a strong focus on developing practical skills that enhance productivity, drive

source_jobs.title: Subject Matter Expert (SME) - AI for Leaders (Late 2026)
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.

Course development schedule

Work on this course will begin in the first half of 2026. We will be recruiting for SME roles on a rolling basis from now until the course enters development, so if you think you're a fit for the role, please fill out an application and we'll be in touch.

About you 

You bring at least 10 years of leadership experience, at least 2+ years of experience in AI business strategy and adoption, and a genuine passion for helping professionals thrive and achieve success. You have a point of view on what leaders need to succeed in applying AI to their business, and you’re able to break concepts down and make them accessible to beginners. As a SME for our online course module, you'll have the opportunity to shape our AI Leadership curriculum, ensure we’re teaching what matters most, share your expertise with career advancers, and guide them toward achieving their goals. 



SME role objectives

 - Create compelling, relevant, industry-aligned courses that give learners what they need to advance in their field

 - Record authentic, engaging videos illustrating AI Leadership concepts and showing learners how to use technologies and tools related to driving results with AI.  

 - Inspire learners, build confidence in their capabilities, and help them understand how to adopt and integrate AI into their leadership practice.

 - Optimize learner engagement, learner retention, and learner success 



Our learners

Our learners are driven professionals from diverse fields. Some are just beginning to explore new career opportunities, while others are looking to deepen their expertise. What they all share is a strong desire to acquire new skills that will help them advance their careers—whether that’s stepping into a new role, earning a promotion, or excelling in their current position.



Our courses

Each week, learners:

 - Complete on-demand online content created by industry experts (that’s you!) featuring scenario-based learning, industry context, expert videos, and best practices (6-7 hours)

 - Apply their skills via a hands-on “Playbook” project that you help create (1-2 hours)

 - Attend a live session to learn from a different industry expert alongside their classmates (2 hours)

 - Get extra support by connecting with classmates on Slack or reaching out to our Learner Success team for support 



As a SME, you’ll collaborate with our Learning Experience Design team to shape the on-demand content and the Playbook, ensuring that the topical coverage, scenarios, videos, and applied skills are relevant and industry-aligned. Our designers will also ask for your input and review on the live session agenda. Most importantly, you’ll record authentic, engaging videos that make learners feel like they have a supportive, savvy guide to AI Leadership.



Your duties and time commitment

 - Overall Time Commitment: 35 - 40 hours over 6 weeks.

 - Week 1: Kickoff meeting with learning experience designer, review and refine module outline, clarify schedule. 

 - Weeks 2 through 5: 1-2 working sessions with learning experience designer. These sessions will focus on mapping out lessons, exercises, and video content. In-between working sessions, you and the designer will work in parallel to flesh out the content and develop detailed outlines for the videos. 

 - Week 6: Record and upload your videos. 

 - Week 7: Review the module and give feedback.

source_jobs.title: Live Session Instructor - AI for Sales
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.


ABOUT THE COURSE

This hands-on course is designed for sales professionals ready to move beyond low-stakes AI use cases and apply AI to the parts of the sales cycle that matter most: prospecting, nurturing leads, crafting proposals, closing deals, and scaling the entire cycle with agentic workflows.

It answers the key questions sales teams are asking today:

 - Where does AI provide the highest impact in the B2B sales funnel?

 - How can AI help find and reach the right accounts faster?

 - How can personalized outreach scale without sounding robotic?

 - How can agentic workflows handle repetitive deal-cycle tasks while preserving buyer trust?

 - How do I ensure content accuracy, data privacy, and ethical use of AI in sales?


ABOUT YOU

You bring:

 - 7+ years of professional B2B or similar sales experience with hands-on ownership of processes across the sales funnel (prospecting, outreach, discovery, proposal, objection handling, or deal management).

 - Demonstrated experience applying AI tools within sales workflows, including generative AI (e.g., ChatGPT), AI-assisted sales platforms, or automation tools (e.g., Hunter, Zapier, Fireflies AI).

 - Ability to teach prompting techniques, responsible AI use, and content-generation best practices with clarity, nuance, and sales-specific examples.

 - Experience guiding learners through hands-on, scenario-based work, especially in Zoom-based environments where live walkthroughs, demos, and interactive activities are central.

 - Comfort working with sales data (prospect lists, CRM records, engagement signals) and using AI tools to enrich, score, and act on that data.

 - Familiarity with building AI-powered sales tools (AI projects, agentic workflows, prompt libraries), including writing instructions, loading reference files, and designing human review checkpoints.

 - A strong ethical foundation and commitment to responsible AI use, including data privacy, content accuracy, bias awareness, and human-in-the-loop practices appropriate for sales.

 - A growth mindset and deep curiosity about AI trends, staying current on evolving sales technologies, compliance considerations, and the future of AI in B2B sales.




INSTRUCTOR ROLE OBJECTIVES

 - Fully complete the course in advance, gaining hands-on experience with all tools, workflows, and assignments to provide expert-level support to learners throughout the program.

 - Help learners apply AI for Sales skills through hands-on projects, guided builds, and real-world use cases.

 - Support learners in troubleshooting, refining, and expanding on the course’s core exercises, demos, and activities by drawing from your own experience building them.

 - Inspire learners, build confidence, and foster a strong, practical, and responsible understanding of AI for Sales.

 - Drive engagement, retention, and learner success through active, thoughtful facilitation in a highly interactive, application-based learning environment.




OUR LEARNERS

Our learners are ambitious sales professionals from a wide range of industries and company sizes, motivated to stay current with advancements in AI and apply them to their day-to-day sales work. They include:

 - Account Executives and Senior Account Executives focused on accelerating the top and middle of the funnel

 - Sales Development Representatives and Business Development Representatives looking to scale prospecting a

source_jobs.title: Subject Matter Expert (SME) - AI for Financial Planning & Analysis (Late Summer 2026)
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.

Course development schedule

Work on this course will begin in August 2026. We will be recruiting for SME roles on a rolling basis from now until the course enters development, so if you think you're a fit for the role, please fill out an application and we'll be in touch.

More about this AI for Financial Planning & Analysis course

Finance professionals are under more pressure than ever before. Teams are shrinking, reporting cycles are accelerating, and leadership expects faster, more strategic insight from fewer people. AI is rapidly transforming how financial analysis, forecasting, reporting, and scenario modeling are done, but most finance professionals were never trained to use these tools safely inside corporate environments.

This 5-week program teaches learners how to use generative AI, automation, and modern analytics tools to accelerate core finance workflows, including variance analysis, financial reporting, budget forecasting, and executive summaries. Learners apply AI to their actual work from Week 1, starting with a Corporate AI Policy Navigator that ensures every tool and technique meets compliance and audit requirements.

By the end, learners leave with automated reporting workflows, AI-assisted forecasting models, and the confidence to implement AI across their finance function with no coding required. They earn a university-branded certificate signaling AI fluency in one of the most credential-sensitive professions in the market.

About you 

You bring at least 7 years of FP&A experience, with at least 2 years of experience using AI for reporting, analysis, forecasting, or modeling. You have been a financial analysis professional in private industry, in government, or in consulting. You have a point of view on how established and emerging AI tools can help finance professionals automate or accelerate core workflows, and you’re able to break concepts down and make them accessible to beginners. As a SME for our online course, you'll have the opportunity to shape our AI for FP&A curriculum, ensure we’re teaching what matters most, share your expertise with career advancers, and guide them toward achieving their goals. 



SME role objectives

 - Create compelling, relevant, industry-aligned courses that give learners what they need to advance in their field

 - Record authentic, engaging videos illustrating AI for FP&A concepts and concretely showing learners how to use AI tools to support and execute core FP&A workflows.  

 - Inspire learners, build confidence in their capabilities, and help them understand how to responsibly integrate AI into their practice.

 - Optimize learner engagement, learner retention, and learner success 



Our learners

Our learners are driven professionals from diverse fields. Some are just beginning to explore new career opportunities, while others are looking to deepen their expertise. What they all share is a strong desire to acquire new skills that will help them advance their careers—whether that’s stepping into a new role, earning a promotion, or excelling in their current position.



Our courses

Each week, learners:

 - Complete on-demand online content created by industry experts (that’s you!) featuring scenario-based learning, industry context, expert videos, and best practices (6-7 hours)

 - Apply their skills via a hands-on “Playbook” project that you help create (1-2 hours)

 - Attend a live s

source_jobs.title: Learner Success Advocate - Artificial Intelligence (AI)
source_jobs.description:
About Ziplines

Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.



About Ziplines Education
Ziplines Education is a category-leading education company that partners with universities to deliver industry-focused certificate courses that prepare professionals for today’s digital-first workforce. Each year, we have the opportunity to help thousands of career advancers build the knowledge, skills, and credentials they need to move their careers forward. 92% of learners would recommend our programs, and our net promoter score is consistently in the excellent range. Together, we empower adult learners to confidently pursue the career they’ve always wanted.


About the Team
Our learning team supports and guides our learners throughout their course experience. This team consists of learning designers, learner success advocates, live session instructors, and career coaches. Employees on this team work as part-time contractors. Navigating ambiguity, collaborating with team members, and taking ownership are necessary traits for success in the role. The learning team is essential to the success of Ziplines by supporting learners to achieve success in the course and in their career aspirations.


About the Role
As a Learner Success Advocate for Artificial Intelligence (AI), you’ll serve as the main point of contact and guide for learners enrolled in our AI course —supporting them as they build confidence using AI tools, strengthen prompt and communication skills, and apply AI thoughtfully in real-world contexts. You will work approximately 10-15 hours per week, spanning 5 to 7 weeks.

Your primary goal is to ensure learners stay engaged, feel supported, and successfully complete the program—especially as they navigate the emerging, sometimes ambiguous landscape of AI.


What You’ll Do


LEARNER ENGAGEMENT AND COMMUNICATION

 - Serve as the primary point of contact for your assigned learners across Slack, email, and Zoom.

 - Foster trust and connection while supporting learners in overcoming both technical challenges and emotional hurdles as they navigate the AI landscape.

 - Lead weekly Slack communications, provide motivational updates, and respond promptly to questions.

 - Conduct 1:1 check-ins or small group outreach for at-risk learners, providing personalized support and encouragement.
   

Live Session Support

 - Engage in weekly live sessions as a moderator and co-host, managing Zoom logistics, chat engagement, breakout rooms, and learner support needs.

 - Partner with instructors to create an interactive, high-energy environment that reflects the dynamic nature of the AI space.

 - Identify and address learner experience challenges, including issues related to content clarity, assignment expectations, or access to tools.
   

Course and Monitor Learning

 - Track learner activity, completion of weekly assignments, and participation in both asynchronous and synchronous components.

 - Flag and proactively intervene with at-risk learners, using data from the Learning Management System and communication logs.

 - Maintain updated and organized documentation on each learner’s progress and engagement.
   

About You

 - 2+ years in customer-facing, education, or coaching roles.

 - Solid knowledge of—AI technologies, particularly prompt engineering and tools like ChatGPT, Claude, or similar platforms.

 - Demonstrated ability to learn new tools, troubleshoot common issues, and explain concepts clearly.

 - Strong written a
